senses.definition: a person who sells or trims animal hides to produce leather
examples: The rucker prepared the hides for tanning.
We hired a rucker to process the skins after the hunt.

senses.definition: the act of moving the timbers or loads into a ruck (slang); in sailing, the process of securing a rope or line into a snug coil
examples: The crew spent the afternoon rucking the spare rope.
Rucking the load properly helped balance the vessel.

senses.definition: to form or cause ridges or wrinkles; to wrinkle or crumple
examples: The fabric began to ruckle after washing.
Her nose wrinkled, and her lips ruckled into a frown.

senses.definition: having formed ridges or wrinkles; creased
examples: The ruckled parchment showed the years of handling.
His shirt was ruckled from the tight collar.

senses.definition: plural of ruckle; folds or wrinkles in fabric or skin
examples: The ruckles on the sleeve ruined the look of the jacket.
She noticed ruckles forming on the vintage tablecloth.

senses.definition: a small ridge or wrinkle; a young or small ruckle (less common)
examples: A ruckling appeared along the hem.
The fabric showed a slight ruckling.

senses.definition: a man who handles or deals with rucks (old term, possibly referring to hides or leather processing) or a person who works on a ruck ship equipment; also a surname
examples: The ruckman inspected the hides before tanning.
Local archives mention a ruckman in the tanning guild.

senses.definition: plural of ruckman; multiple workers in leather processing or a group of crew members dealing with rucks
examples: The ruckmen gathered to discuss the tanning process.
Several ruckmen were employed at the workshop.

senses.definition: loads or bags carried on a back or back-mounted gear; also short for rucksack
examples: He shouldered his rucks and started hiking.
Her rucks contained everything she needed for the trip.

senses.definition: a bag carried on the back with two shoulder straps; a backpack
examples: She wore a blue rucksack on the hike.
The rucksack contained her water bottle and map.

senses.definition: plural of rucksack; multiple backpacks
examples: The scouts carried their rucksacks into the bus.
Two rucksacks lay at the entrance.

senses.definition: a small seat or padded support on a ruck (specific equipment or craft term; regional usage)
examples: The ruckseat provided a perch for the craftsman.
Attach the ruckseat to the frame before use.

senses.definition: plural of ruckseat; multiple seats on a ruck-equipped object
examples: The ruckseats were spaced along the platform.
Ruckseats need regular maintenance.

senses.definition: characterized by a rough, wordy, or noisy appearance; slangy in reference to rucks
examples: The room looked rucksey after the party.
He wore a rucksey jacket that hardly matched the mood.

senses.definition: a loud and often noisy disturbance or commotion
examples: There was a huge ruckus in the hallway.
The announcement caused a bit of a ruckus among the crowd.

senses.definition: plural of ruckus; multiple disturbances
examples: The stadium erupted into several ruckuses during the game.
Police quelled a few ruckuses after the concert.

senses.definition: having a rough, uneven, or unreliable quality
examples: The road was rucky and full of potholes.
His memory was a bit rucky that day.

senses.definition: a leafy green plant also known as arugula, used in salads
examples: She added fresh rucola to the salad.
Rucola has a peppery flavor.

senses.definition: plural of rucola; multiple servings of arugula
examples: The burgers were topped with rucolas and tomatoes.
Rucolas were tossed with lemon juice.

senses.definition: abbreviation or plural shorthand; may refer to rucks or rucksacks in informal contexts
examples: Pack your rucs for the trip.
The soldiers loaded their rucs before marching.

senses.definition: the act of belching or a belch sound; a belch
examples: A loud ructation interrupted the quiet.
Ructations can be signs of indigestion.

senses.definition: plural of ructation; belches or belch sounds
examples: She tried to suppress several ructations.
Belching and ructations sometimes occur after carbonated drinks.

senses.definition: a forceful or violent action or event; a fight or upheaval
examples: The ruction between factions disrupted the meeting.
There was a loud ruction over the policy change.

senses.definition: plural of ruction; multiple disputes or fights
examples: There were several ructions during the debate.
Class ructions delayed the plan.

senses.definition: noisy, quarrelsome, or turbulent
examples: The town was ructious after the announcement.
He led a ructious crowd that overwhelmed the organizers.
